memoryArtifacts.headline: FOWLER FAMILY HISTORY FOLLOWS:
memoryArtifacts.description:
FOWLER FAMILY

March 20,  2020
February 14, 2018


 
 
FOWLER FAMILY
GENERATIONS

More before 1400. I stopped at 1400 (Merle Romer.)
	
1. Sir William Fowler of Foxley 1400-1452
1.a. Cecelia Englefiel …

Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Fowler, Fouler, Fowlers, Fouler, Fowlar, Folar, Fouller, Fowlare, Foweller, Fowaller, Foulier, Foullar, Foular and many more. First found in Norfolk where they were seated from very ancient times, some say well before the Norman Conquest and the arrival of Duke William at Hastings in 1066 C.E. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: John Fowler who settled in Virginia in 1622; just two years after the arrival of the "Mayflower." He was followed soon after by settlers of the name in Barbados, Massachusetts, Maryland, and Rhode Island. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Motto Translated: He is wise who watches. This interesting surname is of Anglo-Saxon origin, and is from an occupational name for a bird-catcher or hunter of wild birds. In the medieval period a fowler would have been an important position, and all major houses would have employed one. The derivation is from the Old English pre-7th Century "fugelere", hunter of wild birds. Job-descriptive surnames originally denoted the actual occupation of the name bearer, and later became hereditary. There are several name bearers listed in the "Dictionary of National Biography", many being men of the church, the others tending towards innovation, such as John Fowler (1826 - 1864), who invented the steam plow, in 1858 he received a prize from the Royal Agricultural Society for his steam cultivator, and William Fowler (1761 - 1832), an artist, who was said to have introduced lead lines in representing colored glass. The name reached the New World in the early 17th Century, when it is recorded that one George Fowler, aged 22 yrs., sailed aboard the "Primrose" in July 1635, bound for Virginia. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of Richard Fugelere, which was dated 1218, witness in the "Assize Court Rolls of Lancashire", during the reign of King Henry 2nd, known as "The Frenchman", 1216 - 1272. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當William Fowler於大約1550年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾比士利時，他的父親Thomas Fowler31歲，母親Alexandra Stephens19歲。他於1570年與Ann Heywood在英國結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1626年7月2日在英國德比夏爾達爾波利享年77歲。

nameMeanings.name: Fowler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a fowler, a hunter or trapper of wild birds (a common medieval occupation), from Middle English fogheler, fugheler (Old English fugelere, a derivative of fugol ‘bird’).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When William Fowler was born about 1550, in Bisley, Gloucestershire, England, his father, Thomas Fowler, was 31 and his mother, Alexandra Stephens, was 19. He married Ann Heywood in 1570, in England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 2 July 1626, in Dalbury, Derbyshire, England, at the age of 77.

ja.lifeSummary: William Fowlerが約1550年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアビスリーで生まれた時，父Thomas Fowlerは31歳，母Alexandra Stephensは19歳であった。1570年にイギリスでAnn Heywoodと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1626年7月2日にイギリスデルブイシュアイアダルブリーで77歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Fowler(이)가 대략 1550년 영국 그루스스터실르 비슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Fowler(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Alexandra Stephens(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1570년 영국에서 Ann Heywood(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1626년 7월 2일 영국 더비실르 다버이에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.
